steps.title: Spot the context clue
steps.content: Fox-Foot must prevent any “trace of their passing” from being left. This phrase is key: a “trace” is what's left behind during passage, and he's preventing this because he's being followed. Whatever “trace” means, it must be something discoverable by pursuers.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Evidence” of their passing is exactly what Fox-Foot wants to prevent—the signs or marks that would reveal their route. The other three don't fit: “blemish” is a flaw on an object, “amount” is a quantity, and “sketch” is a drawing. That matches choice A — A is correct.
